THE MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION, CATEGORIES 	 - 17 IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 313. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO 'DEPOSITION ITEM 1'. THE DEPOSITION OF MR. WILLMORE MAY RESUME FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF ASKING THE WITNESS IF HE EVER RECEIVED ANY OTHER CONSTRUCTION CONTRACTS FROM SUB-CONTRACTORS DOING WORK ON HIS PROPERTY AND ANY REASONABLE FOLLOW-UP QUESTIONS. THE DURATION OF FURTHER QUESTIONS RELATED TO THE SPECIFIED QUESTION SHALL NOT EXCEED 30 MINUTES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO 2, 3, 4, AND 5. AFTER COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT STATED THE GROUNDS FOR THE OBJECTION, PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK TO ELICIT A RESPONSE. THE WITNESS WAS NOT INSTRUCTED TO REFUSE TO ANSWER THE SPECIFIED QUESTIONS. This matter was argued. Mr. Burke's oral request for a continuance was denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BRADFORD WILLMORE'S Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security And For Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant From Filing New Litigation In Pro Per is denied. While defendant has established that plaintiff is a vexatious litigant, defendant has failed to show that there is not a reasonable probability that plaintiff will prevail in this litigation. Any motion seeking to prohibit new filings by the plaintiff should be noticed before the Presiding Judge (CCP 391.7). Order signed in open Court. (302/DEW/AY) Judge Diane E. MATTER ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Plaintiff David Grassi's motion to compel responses to deposition questions and to produce documents.
The motion as to request for production of documents #1 - 8 is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with California Rule of Court 335.

The motion to compel production of documents at deposition, categories - 17 is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with California Rule of Court 313. The Court cannot determine whether responsive documents were either withheld or provided based on the moving papers filed by the parties.

The motion to compel further answers to deposition is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to 'deposition item 1'. The deposition of Mr. Willmore may resume for the limited purpose of asking the witness if he ever received any other construction contracts from sub-contractors doing work on his property and any reasonable follow-up questions. The duration of further questions related to the specified question shall not exceed 30 minutes. The motion is denied as to 2, 3, 4, and 5. After counsel for defendant stated the grounds for the objection, plaintiff did not seek to elicit a response. The witness was not instructed to refuse to answer the specified questions.
